arguments.name: blazor-preview
arguments.prompt:
The user wants to see exactly what files would be generated if they create a Blazor Web App called "MyBlazorApp" in the directory ./test-output with interactive server rendering enabled. They do NOT want the project actually created yet — just a dry-run preview showing the file tree that would be produced.

Use `dotnet new blazor` (the Blazor Web App template) with the appropriate parameters for interactive server-side rendering (--interactivity Server) and the project name MyBlazorApp. Use the --dry-run flag to show what would be generated without actually creating files. The output directory should be ./test-output.

Working directory: /tmp/sv-778645b744914e60906f2eec952008a4

arguments.name: blazor-preview

They do NOT want the project actually created yet — just a dry-run preview showing the file tree that would be produced.

Use `dotnet new blazor` (the Blazor Web App template) with the appropriate parameters for interactive server-side rendering (--interactivity Server) and the project name MyBlazorApp. Use the --dry-run flag to show what would be generated without actually creating files. toolCalls.name: task

data.agentDescription: Expert agent for .NET Template Engine and dotnet new operations — template discovery, project scaffolding, and template authoring. Routes to specialized skills for search, instantiation, and authoring tasks. Verifies template-engine domain relevance before deep-diving.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-778645b744914e60906f2eec952008a4 && dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run 2>&1
arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App with Server interactivity

data.content:
Here's the dry-run preview — **no files were created**. Below is the complete file tree that `dotnet new blazor` would generate for **MyBlazorApp** with `--interactivity Server`:

### Project root (`test-output/`)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `MyBlazorApp.csproj` | Project file |
| `Program.cs` | App entry point & service configuration |
| `appsettings.json` | Configuration |
| `appsettings.Development.json` | Dev-specific configuration |

### `Properties/`
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `launchSettings.json` | Dev launch profiles (ports, URLs) |

### `Components/` — Blazor UI
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `App.razor` | Root component (HTML head, scripts) |
| `Routes.razor` | Router setup |
| `_Imports.razor` | Global `@using` directives |
| **Layout/** | |
| `MainLayout.razor` + `.css` | Page shell layout |
| `NavMenu.razor` + `.css` + `.js` | Sidebar navigation |
| `ReconnectModal.razor` + `.css` + `.js` | SignalR reconnection UI (Server interactivity) |
| **Pages/** | |
| `Home.razor` | Landing page |
| `Counter.razor` | Interactive counter demo |
| `Weather.razor` | Weather data page |
| `Error.razor` | Error display page |
| `NotFound.razor` | 404 page |

### `wwwroot/` — Static assets
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `app.css` | App stylesheet |
| `favicon.png` | Browser icon |
| `lib/bootstrap/dist/css/*` | Bootstrap CSS (18 files: full, grid, reboot, utilities — each with RTL, min, and sourcemap variants) |
| `lib/bootstrap/dist/js/*` | Bootstrap JS (8 files: bundle, ESM, standard — each with min and sourcemap variants) |

### Post-creation action
A `dotnet restore` would run automatically after creation to restore NuGet packages.

---

**Key observation:** Because you chose `--interactivity Server`, the template includes `ReconnectModal.razor` (with its `.css` and `.js`) — this handles the UI for when the SignalR circuit disconnects, which is specific to Blazor Server rendering. A `None` or `WebAssembly` interactivity mode would not include these files.

When you're ready to actually create the project, just say the word and I'll run the same command without `--dry-run`.

data.content:
Here's the dry-run preview — **no files were created**:

```
test-output/
├── MyBlazorApp.csproj
├── Program.cs
├── appsettings.json
├── appsettings.Development.json
├── Properties/
│   └── launchSettings.json
├── Components/
│   ├── App.razor
│   ├── Routes.razor
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor / .css
│   │   ├── NavMenu.razor / .css / .js
│   │   └── ReconnectModal.razor / .css / .js  ← Server-specific
│   └── Pages/
│       ├── Home.razor
│       ├── Counter.razor
│       ├── Weather.razor
│       ├── Error.razor
│       └── NotFound.razor
└── wwwroot/
    ├── app.css
    ├── favicon.png
    └── lib/bootstrap/dist/{css,js}/ (26 files)
```

**Notable:** The `ReconnectModal` component (with `.css`/`.js`) is included specifically because of `--interactivity Server` — it handles SignalR reconnection UI. A `dotnet restore` would also run automatically after creation.

Say the word when you're ready to create it for real.
